question_text: The Moon orbits Earth in approximately 28 days. If the Moon is at a certain position today, roughly how far along its orbit will it be in 7 days?
correct_answer: Approximately one-quarter (25%) of the way around its orbit, or 90 degrees
explanation: The Moon takes 28 days to complete one full orbit. In 7 days, it travels 7/28 = 1/4 of its orbit. This is why we see the Moon's phase change from new to first quarter in about one week.
